description: A/B Testing: Compare SLM models for code analysis

testCases.name: Refactor Payment Processing
testCases.code: export function processPayment(userId: string, amount: number, cardToken: string, cvv: string, expiryDate: string, customerEmail: string, customerName: string, billingAddress: string, shippingAddress: string, metadata: Record<string, string>): Promise<PaymentResult> { validateAmount(amount); validateToken(cardToken); validateCVV(cvv); validateExpiry(expiryDate); const encryptedCard = encryptCardData(cardToken, cvv, expiryDate); const gateway = new PaymentGateway(process.env.GATEWAY_KEY!); const chargeId = gateway.charge(encryptedCard, amount, metadata); const receipt = generateReceipt(chargeId, userId, amount, customerEmail, customerName); sendEmail(customerEmail, receipt); logTransaction(userId, chargeId, amount, 'success'); return { chargeId, receipt, status: 'success' }; }
testCases.description: Payment processing violates SRP - too many responsibilities
testCases.expectedTask: Break into separate functions under 24 lines each
testCases.groundTruth: Split into: validatePaymentData(), encryptPaymentInfo(), chargeCard(), sendReceipt(), logTransaction()

testCases.name: Fix Auth Race Condition
testCases.code: async function authenticateUser(email: string, password: string): Promise<User | null> { const user = await db.users.findOne({ email }); if (!user) return null; const isValid = await bcrypt.compare(password, user.passwordHash); if (!isValid) { await db.failedAttempts.create({ email, timestamp: new Date() }); return null; } user.lastLogin = new Date(); await db.users.update(user); const session = await db.sessions.create({ userId: user.id, token: crypto.randomUUID() }); return user; }
testCases.description: Race condition: session may be created twice if concurrent requests arrive
testCases.expectedTask: Fix race condition using database transaction
testCases.groundTruth: Use database transaction for atomicity. Wrap user update and session creation in transaction.

testCases.name: Optimize Database Query
testCases.code: function getAllUsersWithPosts(): User[] { const users = db.users.find({}); const result = []; for (const user of users) { const posts = db.posts.find({ userId: user.id }); const comments = db.comments.find({ userId: user.id }); user.posts = posts; user.comments = comments; result.push(user); } return result; }
testCases.description: N+1 query problem: inefficient database access pattern
testCases.expectedTask: Optimize using JOIN or batch queries
testCases.groundTruth: Use JOIN query or batch load posts/comments in one query with IN clause

testCases.name: Handle Null Safely
testCases.code: function parseUserData(json: string): User { const data = JSON.parse(json); return { id: data.id, name: data.name, email: data.email, phone: data.contact.phone, address: data.contact.address, city: data.contact.address.city }; }
testCases.description: Missing null checks - can crash if data structure incomplete
testCases.expectedTask: Add defensive null checks and use optional chaining
testCases.groundTruth: Use optional chaining (?.) and nullish coalescing (??) or add explicit null checks

testCases.name: Refactor Complex Function
testCases.code:
function generateReport(data: any[]): string { let html = ''; for (let i = 0; i < data.length; i++) { const row = data[i]; let status = 'unknown'; if (row.value > 1000) { if (row.type === 'A') status = 'high'; else if (row.type === 'B') status = 'medium'; } else if (row.value > 500) status = 'medium'; else status = 'low'; html += ``; } html += '
${row.id}${row.name}${status}
'; return html; }
testCases.description: Complex nested conditionals and mixed concerns
testCases.expectedTask: Extract status logic and HTML generation into separate functions
testCases.groundTruth: Create calculateStatus() function, use template or builder for HTML generation

testCases.name: Add Error Handling
testCases.code: function fetchAndParseJSON(url: string) { const response = fetch(url); const json = response.json(); const result = processData(json); saveToDatabase(result); return result; }
testCases.description: No error handling - network, parsing, or DB errors will crash
testCases.expectedTask: Add try-catch with proper error handling and logging
testCases.groundTruth: Wrap fetch, JSON.parse, processData, and saveToDatabase in try-catch. Log and throw meaningful errors.
